competitor_patterns: Massive word counts: top-ranking posts by Outdoorgearlab (16,551w), Bikerumor (19,283w), and Bikkoa (22,719w) dwarf our 1,000–1,245w posts — Google rewards depth and comprehensiveness for these informational queries
Comparison tables are standard: all top-3 competitors use structured HTML comparison tables (has_table: true) to compare multiple products side-by-side — a format we currently use in zero posts

Third-party testing credibility signals: top results prominently feature 'Tested & Ranked', 'Bikerumor Tested', and named reviewer bylines (e.g. Justin Simoni) — lending editorial authority our brand-authored posts lack

Segmented audience H2 structure: leaders break content into rider-type sections (MTB, Road, Budget, Performance) rather than a single product focus — this captures a wider range of long-tail queries within one URL
FAQ + Reviews combined: all top competitors include both FAQ schema and user/editorial reviews in a single post — we have FAQ but zero embedded review content, weakening our E-E-A-T signals
our_gaps: Word count is critically thin: our posts average ~1,150 words vs competitors' 16,000–22,000 words — we cannot rank for competitive head terms like 'best cycling glasses 2026' at position 9 without a 6,000–10,000w comprehensive guide
Zero comparison tables: none of our recent 4 posts include a product comparison table, which is a non-negotiable content element in every top-ranking competitor page for these query types

No third-party or rider testimony: all content is brand-authored with no named testers, rider quotes, or independent review signals — this weakens E-E-A-T and makes our pages look promotional rather than editorial

Single-product focus misses query breadth: every post focuses exclusively on StradaPro, whereas ranking pages cover 5–18 products — searchers on 'best cycling glasses 2026' want a market overview, not a single-brand page

No internal content cluster linking 'alternatives to oakley' (6,852 impressions, 0 clicks): we have zero dedicated page targeting this highest-volume keyword in our GSC data, leaving 6,852 monthly impressions completely unconverted
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — competitor Allsportssunglassesusa ranks #1 for this; StradaPro's click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system is a direct match and we have no dedicated post
lightweight cycling sunglasses — our 25g frame is a standout spec no current post targets directly; this is a high-purchase-intent modifier used by serious road cyclists
road cycling glasses with anti-fog — compound intent keyword combining our two strongest product features; not yet targeted as a standalone URL
best cycling sunglasses under €150 — high commercial intent, our existing budget post targets this concept but doesn't use the exact phrase as a primary keyword in the H1 or meta title
30 day trial cycling glasses — near-zero competition, directly targets our unique risk-reversal offer; likely to appear in AI-generated product recommendation answers
next_post_guidelines: Write a minimum 6,000-word comprehensive guide for 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' — include sections for road, MTB, budget, premium, and anti-fog categories with StradaPro featured in at least 2 sections organically

Include a comparison table in every post going forward: columns should be Weight (g), Lens System, Anti-Fog, UV Rating, Price, Trial Period — this directly showcases StradaPro's advantages at a glance

Add a named 'tested by' byline or rider testimonial block in the first 200 words of each post — even a single quoted customer with a name and ride type (e.g. 'Marco R., road cyclist, Milan') signals real-world experience to Google

Target the 'alternatives to oakley' query (6,852 impressions, 0 clicks) with a dedicated post: title must include that exact phrase, position StradaPro as the lead recommendation, include a spec table vs Oakley Sutro Lite

Segment posts by rider type using H2s: add subheadings like 'Best for Road Cyclists', 'Best for Hot Weather Rides', 'Best for Riders Who Wear Prescription Glasses' — this broadens topical coverage and captures long-tail variants
Embed FAQ schema with a minimum of 6 questions per post — competitor pages with FAQ rich results visually dominate SERPs and increase CTR from position 7–9 by an estimated 30–60%

Include internal links between all anti-fog posts, the budget comparison post, and any future 'alternatives to Oakley' post — build a topical cluster so Google understands Velluto's authority across cycling eyewear subtopics
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table (HTML table or styled div) to every post immediately — include at minimum 3 competing products plus StradaPro, with Weight, Lens Interchangeability, Anti-Fog, and Price columns; this alone can improve dwell time and ranking for transactional queries

Rewrite all meta titles to lead with the target keyword and include a differentiating hook within 60 characters — current titles front-load the feature angle rather than the search query, reducing relevance signals for the exact match query

Add an 'About the Author / How We Tested' block (50–80 words) to every existing and future post — this directly addresses Google's E-E-A-T (Experience) requirement and differentiates brand content from thin affiliate pages
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (6,852 impressions, 0 CTR, pos 7.1) → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Velluto | Why: Leads with exact query phrase 'Oakley Alternatives', adds year for freshness signal, brand for trust — current page likely has no dedicated URL for this query at all; a new dedicated page is the priority fix

QUERY: 'veluto' (323 impressions, 0.6% CTR, pos 5.7) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses | Official Store | Why: 'Veluto' is a misspelling of Velluto — the homepage/brand page meta title should include the misspelling variant in the meta description (not title) and ensure the title clearly signals official brand destination to capture navigational intent clicks

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (166 impressions, 2.4% CTR, pos 7.1) → New title: Oakley Alternatives: StradaPro vs Sutro Lite 2026 | Why: Direct comparison framing raises click curiosity, exact query match in position 1 of title, product name adds specificity that generic titles lack

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (166 impressions, 1.8% CTR, pos 9.3) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: Mirrors the exact title format of #1 Outdoorgearlab result ('Tested & Ranked') which signals editorial credibility; year in title matches searcher intent for fresh results

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (55 impressions, 1.8% CTR, pos 7.4) → New title: The Oakley Alternative Road Cyclists Are Switching To | Why: Curiosity-gap framing drives clicks at position 7; implies social proof ('switching to') without making a false claim; targets singular 'alternative' exact match
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (pos 7.1, 6,852 impressions, 0 clicks) → Topic: 'Best Alternatives to Oakley Cycling Sunglasses in 2026' | Add: Dedicated 4,000+ word post with H2 sections per competitor alternative (Roka, 100%, Tifosi, Velluto), a spec comparison table (Weight vs Oakley Sutro Lite, lens system, price, trial policy), and a CTA block for the 30-day trial — this is the single highest-ROI content action given 6,852 impressions currently generating zero clicks

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 9.3, 166 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Our Tested Picks by Ride Type' | Add: Expand existing or create new 8,000w guide with named reviewer, comparison table of 8–10 glasses including StradaPro, separate H2 sections for road/MTB/budget/premium, FAQ with 8 questions — targets the exact format of pos 1 Outdoorgearlab page to push from 9.3 into top 5

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.6, 131 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, MTB & Budget Picks Compared' | Add: Merge with 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' content into a single authoritative guide (Google treats these as near-identical intent); add structured data for Article and FAQPage schema, include a 'Quick Picks' jump table at the top mirroring Outdoorgearlab's format

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.1, 166 impressions) → Topic: Consolidate with 'alternatives to oakley' into one canonical URL targeting all variants | Add: Interlink from homepage and product page; include schema markup with product comparisons; add user-generated quote comparing StradaPro to their previous Oakley

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 8.1, 22 impressions) → Topic: Ensure the main 2026 guide above targets this evergreen (non-year) variant in the meta description and intro paragraph | Add: An evergreen intro paragraph that reads naturally without the year so the page retains relevance past 2026 — this prevents the page from going stale and maintains ranking momentum into 2027
geo_angles: Position StradaPro as the answer to 'What are the best lightweight cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses?' — create a dedicated FAQ-style page or blog section with a direct, quotable answer (e.g. 'At 25g with tool-free click-in lenses and a 30-day trial, the Velluto StradaPro is the most cited lightweight alternative to Oakley among European road cyclists in 2026') — AI systems like Perplexity and Google AI Overviews extract verbatim quotable sentences from pages that directly answer a question

Build a 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Brand]' comparison cluster (Oakley, Roka, 100%, Tifosi) — AI answer engines heavily cite comparison content because it resolves multi-option queries in one place; each post should include a structured verdict paragraph with the phrase 'For cyclists who want X, the StradaPro wins because...' which is the format AI systems extract for recommendations

Publish a dedicated 'Why Velluto Offers a 30-Day Risk-Free Trial' page with schema markup (Offer, FAQPage) — this unique policy is a factual differentiator that AI systems surface when answering 'which cycling glasses have a free trial' or 'best cycling glasses to try before buying'; currently no competitor prominently claims this, making it a zero-competition AI citation opportunity
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Outdoorgearlab — angle: 'We Read the Outdoorgearlab Cycling Glasses Test. Here's What They Missed' — headline idea: 'What the Outdoorgearlab 2026 Cycling Glasses Review Doesn't Tell You About Weight and Lens Systems' — key differentiator: editorial review sites test what's sent to them; Velluto leads with the 25g spec and interchangeable lens system that mass-market reviews underweight — Velluto advantage to lead with: 'Every glass Outdoorgearlab tested weighs over 28g. The StradaPro at 25g is lighter than their top pick, at a third of the price'

COMPETITOR: Oakley — angle: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: The €300 Question' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is €300 Extra Worth It for Road Cyclists?' — key differentiator: direct spec-for-spec table (weight, lens interchangeability, anti-fog, UV400, trial policy, price) shows StradaPro matches or beats Sutro Lite on every functional metric — Velluto advantage to lead with: '30-day risk-free trial vs Oakley's no-trial policy — test it on your actual rides before you decide'

COMPETITOR: Tifosi — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Budget King vs Smart Mid-Premium' — headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Cycling Glasses Win for Serious Road Riders?' — key differentiator: Tifosi competes on price but lacks Italian design, dedicated anti-fog system, and the 30-day trial — Velluto advantage to lead with: 'The StradaPro costs more than the Tifosi Rail — here's exactly what you get for the difference, and why 30 days of real riding beats any shop trial'
